label: Hand rubbing blister without infection or burn red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated hand rubbing blister.
No infection, burn pathway, widespread blistering, medication reaction, mucosal involvement, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Cellulitis, abscess, pus, open infected wound, fever, systemic illness, burn, chemical/electrical injury, widespread blistering, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction concern, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, wound care, or the ED if the blister is not healing or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports blister framing, protection from rubbing, simple skin care, and escalation for infection, burns, widespread blistering, mucosal lesions, or severe skin reactions. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated hand rubbing blister with infection, burn, high-risk skin, and red flags absent.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports blister framing, protection from rubbing, simple skin care, and escalation for infection, burns, widespread blistering, mucosal lesions, or severe skin reactions. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated hand rubbing blister with infection, burn, high-risk skin, and red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated hand rubbing blister
no burn or severe skin reaction
